title: salix niphoclada var. muriei
intro: Salix niphoclada var. muriei, also known as Mountain Willow, is a shrub or small tree species in the willow family (Salicaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Mountain Willow include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Mountain Willow can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for wound healing and skin issues.
